current.summary: Generated 16 normative test vectors + 2 meta hash vectors for ACP spec. Completed 5 rounds of iterative review fixing derived_from semantics, algorithm unification (7.3 universal), source format clarity, and provenance chain coherence. Ran 5-persona adversarial discussion panel.

activeWork.description: ACP spec v0.1.0-draft is review-complete. 5 rounds of iterative fixes applied, 5-persona adversarial panel executed and documented. Two small mechanical fixes remain before tagging, plus known-limitations documentation from panel findings.
activeWork.progress: Spec body is architecturally sound — panel found zero structural design flaws. All test vectors use real hashes forming a coherent provenance chain (TV-MH → TV-MH2). Committed as 794b90c. Adversarial panel results documented in adversarial-discussion-panel.md (uncommitted). Panel identified YAML-to-JSON type mapping as the #1 interoperability gap across all 5 reviewers.

git.lastCommit: 794b90c docs: ACP specification v0.1.0-draft with normative test vectors

recentDecisions: Section 7.3 (directory tree) is the universal content_hash algorithm — 7.2 demoted to sub-procedure, eliminating publisher/consumer ambiguity
compose removed from per-entry relation enum — composition implied by list length > 1
derived_from.source is a human-readable hint, source_hash is the authoritative cross-reference anchor
Empty content directories MUST be rejected as unpublishable
Symlink cycles MUST be detected and rejected
Version reset on ALL derivation types (fork, convert, adapt, composition)
TV-MH and TV-MH2 form a coherent provenance chain using real hashes from other test vectors
nextSteps: Fix TV-15 per-file hash prefix inconsistency (uses sha256: prefix but 7.2 outputs bare hex)
Fix schema example source_hash placeholder (sha256:x9y8z7w6... contains non-hex chars)
Address panel's #1 finding: add normative YAML-to-JSON type mapping table to Section 8
Constrain published_at to RFC 3339 profile (mandatory UTC Z suffix, seconds precision)
Add unknown fields handling rule (include in meta_hash or strip — must be explicit)
Add signing input test vector for Section 9.1
Document known limitations from panel findings in Security Considerations
Consider committing adversarial-discussion-panel.md and updated spec/generator
Run /spec-writing audit as final check before tagging v0.1.0-draft

context.notes:
KEY FILES:

1. docs/spec/skills/acp-spec.md — THE SPEC (v0.1.0-draft, post-5-round review)
2. docs/spec/skills/generate_test_vectors.py — Reference implementation for hash algorithms (16 TVs + 2 meta hash TVs)
3. docs/spec/skills/provenance.md — DESIGN DOC (full decision history, D1-D12)
4. docs/spec/skills/llm-feedback.md — External review (Claude + Gemini, 2 rounds each)
5. docs/spec/skills/adversarial-discussion-panel.md — 5-persona adversarial review results (UNCOMMITTED)

ADVERSARIAL PANEL SUMMARY:
- 5 agents: Security Researcher, Standards Expert, Implementer, Red Team, Technical Auditor
- Consensus #1 finding: YAML-to-JSON type mapping unspecified (all 5 flagged independently)
- 16 attack scenarios identified by Red Team — NONE require breaking a signature
- Key insight: ACP is a provenance spec, not a trust spec. Gap between 'hashes verify' and 'content is trustworthy' must be clearly communicated.
- Triage checklist in adversarial-discussion-panel.md with 4 priority levels

POST-REVIEW CHANGES (uncommitted in acp-spec.md):
- Multiple iterative fixes from rounds 2-5 of review
- These are uncommitted because the adversarial panel may prompt further changes
- The committed version (794b90c) has the test vectors but not the final review fixes

IMPORTANT: acp-spec.md has uncommitted changes from review rounds. The committed version at 794b90c is the pre-review-fix state. Run git diff docs/spec/skills/acp-spec.md to see what changed.

previousSessions.summary: Designed and wrote the Agent Content Provenance (ACP) specification — portable meta.yaml sidecar format for AI coding tool content provenance with dual hashes, Sigstore/SSH signing, and lineage tracking.
completedWork: ACP spec v0.1.0 draft complete with RFC 2119 language, conformance classes, security considerations
Full design cycle: brainstorm → content type mapping → adversarial panels → external review → spec writing
Two rounds of external review feedback incorporated (Claude + Gemini)
nextSteps: Generate inline test vectors for Appendix B

previousSessions.summary: Design provenance and attestation model for syllago content types — authorship, versioning, source binding, derivation lineage, content hashing.
completedWork: Extensive prior research and adversarial review. Prior panel recommended 8-field minimum sidecar convention.
nextSteps: Map content types to provenance needs
